entityName: March comes in like a lion Season 2

metadata.entityName: March comes in like a lion Season 2

ingredients.name: Tsukudaya Liquor Store
ingredients.entityContext: This small liquor shop in the Tsukuda neighborhood appears in the background of scenes depicting Rei Kiriyama's daily life, grounding his solitary existence in the quiet streets near his apartment.

ingredients.name: Tsukudao Bridge
ingredients.address: Tsukudao Bridge, 大橋 Tsukuda, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0044, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Rei frequently crosses this bridge on his walks between the Kawamoto family shop and his apartment, and the river view becomes a visual motif for his emotional isolation slowly giving way to connection.

ingredients.name: Hikarigaoka Library
ingredients.localName: 練馬区立光が丘図書館
ingredients.address: Hikarigaoka Library, 4 Chome-1-5 Hikarigaoka, Nerima City, Tokyo 179-0072, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Nerima public library appears as a study location visited during slice-of-life scenes in Season 2, reflecting the quieter moments of characters seeking refuge in books.

ingredients.name: Ishikawajima Memorial Hospital
ingredients.address: Ishikawajima Memorial Hospital, 2 Chome-5-2 Tsukuda, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This hospital near the Tsukuda area appears in Season 2 in connection with scenes where characters deal with health concerns, reinforcing the weight of adult responsibility pressing on Rei's world.

ingredients.name: Sendagaya Kitchen
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒151-0051 Tokyo, Shibuya, Sendagaya, 1 Chome−7−12 千駄ヶ谷厨房
ingredients.entityContext: Located near the Tokyo Shogi Hall in Sendagaya, this Western restaurant appears as a meal spot for shogi players, capturing the ordinary downtime professional players spend between grueling tournament rounds.

ingredients.name: Kansai Shogi Hall shogi dojo
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒569-1123 Osaka, Takatsuki, Akutagawacho, 2 Chome−2−6 関西将棋会館
ingredients.entityContext: The Kansai Shogi Hall in Osaka is the setting for Rei's away tournament matches in Season 2 Episode 9, where he faces off against formidable Kansai-based professionals in a charged competitive atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Jankara JR Fukushima(KARAOKE)
ingredients.localName: ジャンカラ JR福島店
ingredients.address: Jankara JR Fukushima(KARAOKE), 堀内ビル 1～5F 7 Chome-12-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward, Osaka, 553-0003, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Osaka karaoke venue near the shogi hall appears in the Kansai arc as a place where players and their support entourages unwind after tournament rounds, showing the lighter social side of the shogi world.

ingredients.name: Matsusaka M Umeda Fukushima Branch
ingredients.localName: Mの焼肉 福島本店 | Matsusaka Gyu Yakiniku M umeda fukushima branch
ingredients.address: Matsusaka M Umeda Fukushima Branch, 6 Chome-3-7 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward, Osaka, 553-0003, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Osaka yakiniku restaurant appears in the Kansai tournament episodes as a post-match dining spot, providing a glimpse of how professional shogi players celebrate or decompress after a long match day.

ingredients.name: General Taxi Rank, Kyoto Station Karasuma Entrance
ingredients.localName: 京都駅烏丸口 一般タクシーのりば
ingredients.address: General Taxi Rank, Kyoto Station Karasuma Entrance, Higashishiokojicho, Shimogyo Ward, Kyoto, 600-8216, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Kyoto Station taxi stand appears briefly as characters arrive or depart during the Kyoto shogi-related excursions in Episode 9, marking transitions in the travel narrative of that arc.

ingredients.name: Somedono-in Temple
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒604-8042 Kyoto, Nakagyo Ward, 新Somedono-in Temple, 四条上中之町５６２
ingredients.entityContext: This small Buddhist temple in central Kyoto appears in the Kyoto stroll sequence of Episode 9, part of the atmospheric backdrop as characters explore the city between tournament commitments.

ingredients.name: Cardpro Kyoto
ingredients.localName: カードプロ 京都店
ingredients.address: Cardpro Kyoto, 513 Higashigawacho, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-8046, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Kyoto trading card shop appears in a brief background scene during the Kyoto arc in Episode 9, adding local color to the street-level exploration of Kyoto's commercial district.

ingredients.name: Mokumeganeya Kyoto
ingredients.address: Mokumeganeya Kyoto, イーグルコート京都錦小路 1F 444 Jumonjicho, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-8121, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This artisan jewelry shop on Nishiki-koji in Kyoto appears during the Kyoto episodes, representing the refined craft culture of the city that contrasts with the intense mental battles of the shogi tournament.

ingredients.name: Shijo Bridge
ingredients.address: Shijo-dori, Kyoto, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The famous Shijo Bridge over the Kamo River in Kyoto appears as a landmark backdrop in the Kyoto arc of Episode 9, with its lively street scene framing the characters' journey through the old capital.

ingredients.name: Yamafuku
ingredients.localName: 先斗町 鍋料理 あごつゆしゃぶしゃぶ山福
ingredients.address: Yamafuku, 187 Zaimokucho, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-8017, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Pontocho shabu-shabu restaurant in Kyoto appears in the Kyoto tournament arc, where it serves as a dining backdrop capturing the elegance of Kyoto's historic dining alley alongside the shogi storyline.

ingredients.name: Tsukuda Namiyoke Inari Daimyōjin & Osaki Inari Jinja
ingredients.address: Namiyoke Shrine, 6 Chome-20-37 Tsukiji, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0045, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Tsukiji shrine near Rei's neighborhood appears in Episode 10 as part of the Tsukuda street environment, with its festive character reflecting the local community life that Rei gradually comes to appreciate.

ingredients.name: Morioka Station
ingredients.address: Morioka Station, 1-48 Moriokaekimaedori, Morioka, Iwate 020-0034, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Morioka Station serves as the arrival point for Rei and his shogi rivals during the Morioka tournament arc in Episode 14, with its distinctive facade marking the beginning of an important away match.

ingredients.name: Hotel Taikan
ingredients.address: Hotel Taikan, Yunotate-37-1 Tsunagi, Morioka, Iwate 020-0055, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Tsunagi onsen hotel outside Morioka is where Rei stays during the Episode 14 tournament, and the hot-spring setting provides a rare moment of physical rest against the emotional weight of the competition.

ingredients.name: Sendai Station
ingredients.address: Sendai Station, 1 Chome-1-1 Central, Aoba Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 980-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sendai Station is the hub of the Sendai tournament arc spanning Episodes 15-16, where Rei travels to compete and where the series depicts the regional shogi tournament circuit with quiet documentary detail.

ingredients.name: Sendai Station rooftop parking
ingredients.address: Sendai Station rooftop parking, 1 Chome-1-1 Central, Aoba Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 980-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The rooftop parking lot above Sendai Station appears as a vantage point in the Sendai arc, offering a panoramic view of the city that underscores Rei's sense of solitude while competing far from home.

ingredients.name: Zenjiro - Itsutsubashi-yokocho Branch annex
ingredients.localName: たんや善治郎 五橋横丁別館
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒980-0021 Miyagi, Sendai, Aoba Ward, Central, 1 Chome−1−8 Zenjiro - Itsutsubashi-yokocho Branch annex, 杜の都 五橋横丁
ingredients.entityContext: This Sendai gyutan restaurant appears in Episode 15 as a local meal spot during the Sendai tournament trip, grounding the arc in the regional food culture of Miyagi Prefecture.

ingredients.name: Taimuzu JR Sendai Eki Nishiguchi
ingredients.address: Taimuzu JR Sendaiekinishiguchi, 1 Chome-1 Central, Aoba Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 980-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This parking lot near Sendai Station's west exit appears as a transitional setting in the Sendai tournament episodes, part of the realistic urban geography the anime carefully maps.

ingredients.name: Toyoko Inn Sendai Station West Central
ingredients.address: Toyoko Inn Sendai-eki Nishi-guchi Chuo, 1 Chome-1-10 Central, Aoba Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 980-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This business hotel near Sendai Station is where Rei lodges during the Episode 16 Sendai tournament segment, its utilitarian atmosphere reflecting the no-frills reality of life on the professional shogi circuit.

ingredients.name: Mos Premium Sendagaya
ingredients.localName: モスプレミアム 千駄ヶ谷店
ingredients.address: Mos Premium, 1 Chome-8-１１ Sendagaya, Shibuya, Tokyo 151-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Mos Burger near the Shogi Hall in Sendagaya appears in Episode 16 as a casual food stop for Rei and other characters associated with the shogi world, blending the mundane and the professional.

ingredients.name: Tenseien
ingredients.address: Tenseien, 682 Yumoto, Hakone, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 250-0311, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Hakone onsen ryokan is where Rei and the Kawamoto sisters stay during the Hakone trip in Episode 17, a pivotal restorative episode that gives Rei emotional breathing room between intense tournament arcs.

ingredients.name: Monja Taro
ingredients.address: Monja Tarō, 1 Chome-22-1-103 Tsukishima, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0052, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Tsukishima monjayaki restaurant appears in Episode 22, reflecting the island neighborhood's identity as Tokyo's monja street and grounding Rei's life in the distinctive food culture of his local area.

ingredients.name: Asashio Bridge
ingredients.address: Asashio Bridge, 2 Chome-3 Tsukishima, 中央区 東京都 104-0052, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This bridge between Tsukishima and the waterfront appears in the opening sequence and in atmospheric shots throughout the series, representing Rei's solitary commutes through the reclaimed land of his home district.

ingredients.name: Sumiyoshi Bridge
ingredients.address: Sorihashi Bridge - Sumiyoshi Taisha, 2 Chome-9-89 Sumiyoshi, Sumiyoshi Ward, Osaka, 558-0045, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic arched Sori-hashi bridge at Sumiyoshi Taisha in Osaka appears in the opening credits sequence, its graceful curve providing one of the anime's most visually distinctive location shots.

ingredients.name: Nobu-an
ingredients.entityContext: This soba restaurant in the Tsukuda neighborhood appears in opening sequence scenes as part of the authentic old-town streetscape that defines Rei's home district in the anime.

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Tsukishima 3-chome
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 中央月島三丁目店
ingredients.address: FamilyMart Chuo Tsukishima 3-chome, 3 Chome-12-4 Tsukishima, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0052, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This convenience store in Tsukishima appears in the opening credits as part of the realistic urban texture of Rei's neighborhood, capturing the mundane landmarks of his solitary daily routines.

ingredients.name: Chuo Kuritsu Tsukudanichomenishi Park
ingredients.localName: 中央区立佃ニ丁目西公園
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-11-4 Tsukuda, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small park in the Tsukuda neighborhood appears in Episode 3 as one of the quiet local spaces near Rei's apartment, reinforcing the lived-in atmosphere of his low-key residential district.

ingredients.name: Dai 2 Kashima Liquor Store
ingredients.address: Dai 2 Kashima Liquor Store, 2 Chome-20-4 Tsukuda, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This local liquor shop in Tsukuda appears in the opening sequence as part of the authentic neighborhood scenery, showing the small independent shops that give Rei's home area its shitamachi character.

ingredients.name: Asakusa Hanayashiki
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-28-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Japan's oldest amusement park appears in Episode 5 when Rei accompanies the Kawamoto sisters on an outing, the retro charm of its old rides perfectly matching the warm, nostalgic tone of the Kawamoto family scenes.

ingredients.name: Chuo-Ohashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Tsukuda Ohashi Bridge, Chuo City, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Tsukuda bridge spanning the Sumida River appears in Episode 1 as part of the establishing shots of Rei's neighborhood, its crossing a repeated visual marker of his movements between the island and the wider city.

ingredients.name: Ueno Station
ingredients.address: 7 Chome Ueno, Taito City, Tokyo 110-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Ueno Station appears in Episode 14 as a departure point for Rei's journey to the Morioka tournament, its busy platforms conveying the start of another demanding trip on the professional shogi circuit.

ingredients.name: Tsukuda-Ohashi Bridge
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-8-1 Tsukuda, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This bridge in the Tsukuda area recurs in Episode 1 as a defining landmark of Rei's home district, its waterfront view and old-town surroundings establishing the quiet character of his everyday environment.

ingredients.name: Near the Tsukuda Machikado Museum
ingredients.localName: 佃まちかど展示館付近
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-2 Tsukuda, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The streets near the Tsukuda neighborhood museum appear in Episode 4 as background scenery for Rei's walks through his old-fashioned residential district, full of historic wooden buildings that the anime renders in loving detail.

ingredients.name: Tokyo Metropolitan Gymnasium
ingredients.address: Tokyo Metropolitan Gymnasium, 1 Chome-17-1 Sendagaya, Shibuya, Tokyo 151-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Tokyo Metropolitan Gymnasium in Sendagaya appears in Episode 21 as a backdrop near the shogi association, its grand architecture marking the institutional weight of professional sports culture in the Sendagaya district.

ingredients.name: Brillant Tsukuda
ingredients.address: Brillant Tsukuda, 1 Chome-2-1 Tsukuda, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This cafe in the Tsukuda neighborhood appears in Episode 4 as part of the local streetscape near Rei's apartment, one of the small businesses that populate his quiet island district.

ingredients.name: Tsukishima Sta.
ingredients.address: 2-chōme-10 Tsukishima, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0052, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tsukishima Station serves as Rei's home subway stop in Episode 7, the gateway to and from his island neighborhood that marks the boundary between his insular world and the demands of the broader city.

ingredients.name: Echizenbori Drugstore (since 1923)
ingredients.address: Echizenbori Drugstore (since 1923), 2 Chome-17-12 Shinkawa, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This century-old pharmacy in Shinkawa near Tsukuda appears in Episode 1 as a street-level landmark conveying the historic, working-class character of Rei's home district in central Tokyo.

ingredients.name: Hakone-Yumoto Station
ingredients.address: Hakone-Yumoto Station, Yumoto, Hakone, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 250-0311, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hakone-Yumoto Station is the arrival point for Rei and the Kawamoto sisters in Episode 2, the entry to the hot-spring resort town and the start of a rare lighthearted getaway from the pressures of tournament shogi.

ingredients.name: Yumoto Fujiya Hotel
ingredients.address: Yumoto Fujiya Hotel, 256-1 Yumoto, Hakone, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 250-0392, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This historic hotel near Hakone-Yumoto Station appears in Episode 2 as the accommodation for the Kawamoto sisters' Hakone trip, its traditional Japanese inn atmosphere marking the episode as a warm respite episode.

ingredients.name: Shogi Hall
ingredients.localName: 日本将棋連盟本部 将棋会館
ingredients.address: Shogi Hall, 2 Chome-39-9 Sendagaya, Shibuya, Tokyo 151-0051, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Japan Shogi Federation headquarters in Sendagaya is the nerve center of Rei's professional life, appearing repeatedly throughout Season 2 as the venue where key matches, title matches, and shogi society drama unfold.
